codeReview.summary: Implementation provides version history tracking and rollback capability for memory entries. The feature adds MemoryVersion struct for version history, RollbackAuditEntry for audit trail, RollbackResult for operation results, and comprehensive methods for version management (save_version, get_versions, get_version, rollback_to_version, get_rollback_history, get_latest_version, has_version_history, delete_versions_before). Schema initialization for memory_versions and rollback_audit_log tables is in lib.rs init_schema method. All 10 tests pass, clippy passes with no warnings, and build succeeds.

issues.description: Using unwrap() on serde_json::to_string(&metadata) could panic if metadata contains non-serializable types. Consider using map_err to convert the serialization error into a SwellError.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the Rust skill correctly, producing idiomatic async Rust code with proper error handling via the SwellError type. No deviations from skill procedure were noted.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows standard Rust async patterns with sqlx, proper Result return types, and error mapping throughout.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature uses the same SQL schema initialization pattern (adding CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS in init_schema) as other memory modules. This is consistent with the existing codebase conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: lib.rs init_schema method contains all schema initializations including the new memory_versions and rollback_audit_log tables at lines 306-360.

summary: Feature implementation reviewed. Code is well-structured with proper version history tracking, rollback capability, and audit trail. Schema initialization correctly placed in lib.rs init_schema. Implementation fully addresses the expected behavior: (1) Version history maintained per memory via memory_versions table, (2) Rollback to specific version supported via rollback_to_version(), (3) Audit trail kept via rollback_audit_log. All verification steps pass (build, clippy, tests). One non-blocking issue noted: unwrap() on JSON serialization in save_version could be converted to proper error handling.
